name: Exosome Repair Serum
hero: Cosmetic-grade exosomes at retail. The science is exciting in injectable / clinical settings — the topical evidence is thin, the regulatory ground is unsettled, and the dose claims are unverifiable. Save your money.

scoreBreakdown.n: Exosomes are large vesicles; topical penetration through intact stratum corneum is questionable.
Almost all positive data comes from injectable, not topical, exosomes.
Cosmetic-grade exosome supply chains lack standardised QC.
₹ 1,499 for unverifiable active is poor value.

ingredients.note: Concentration not disclosed; topical evidence absent.
The one ingredient definitely doing something.

useCases.b: There isn't one we can recommend on evidence grounds.
Treat it as an expensive humectant. Don't displace a proven retinoid / SPF / niacinamide for it.
If exosomes prove out in topical RCTs, formulas will be reformulated and prices will move. Today is too early.

alts.name: Niacinamide 5% + HA
alts.note: 1/3 the price; real evidence base; daily-use safe.

alts.name: Buffet (peptide complex)
alts.note: Comparable 'modern serum' positioning; more conservative claims.

alts.name: Hyalu B5 Serum
alts.note: Hydrator + B5 in a vehicle backed by clinical research.

faq.q: Aren't exosomes the next big thing?
faq.a: In injectable post-procedure use — possibly. In a topical retail serum — very unlikely at present formulation standards. Don't conflate the two.
Products where marketing claims significantly exceed the topical evidence base. D doesn't mean 'will harm you' — it means 'won't deliver what's promised at this price'.
Not on a tolerability basis. The concern is opportunity cost — the money would do more elsewhere.
If the goal is anti-ageing: SPF + retinoid + niacinamide. If the goal is hydration: hyaluronic + ceramide moisturiser. The basics, dosed correctly.

sources.n: Kalluri R, LeBleu VS. The biology, function, and biomedical applications of exosomes. Science 2020.
Kim YJ et al. Exosomes derived from stem cells in skin: current status. Cells 2020.
FDA. Important warnings related to use of exosome products. 2019 statement.
EMA. Reflection paper on extracellular vesicles. 2023.
